description_long: This sculpture depicts a group of bears in a dynamic, naturalistic scene, with one large bear sitting upright and two smaller bears interacting around it. The figures are rendered in a rough, textured style, emphasizing the fur and natural forms of the animals. The bears are positioned on a rocky base, which itself sits atop a rectangular plinth, giving the piece a sense of elevation and prominence. The surface is weathered and uneven, suggesting it is made from stone or concrete, and the coloration is predominantly a muted, earthy brown with hints of mossy green. The rear of the sculpture is less detailed, focusing on the rocky backdrop rather than the animals. This type of sculpture is commonly found in public parks or urban plazas, serving as both an artistic focal point and a celebration of wildlife.
description: A textured stone sculpture of a bear family on a rocky base, featuring detailed animal forms and a weathered, earthy finish.
description_view_0: The left side view highlights the upright bear and the layered rocky base, with the smaller bears partially visible.
description_view_1: The rear view shows the rough, rocky backdrop with minimal detail on the animals, focusing on the naturalistic texture.
description_view_2: The right side view displays the seated bear and the arrangement of the smaller bears, emphasizing the depth of the rocky base.
description_view_3: The front view clearly presents the bear family, with the upright bear and two smaller bears in a lively, interactive pose.
attribute_subject_matter: bear family in a naturalistic rocky setting
attribute_location_context: No specific context is visible, but the sculpture appears suited for outdoor urban or park environments.
